gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: ETH burn interval accumulation cap enables full-pool incentive extraction via swapETHForTorusAndBurn after 20 days of inactivity
candidate.claimed_root_cause: _calculateIntervalsETHBurn caps _totalAmountForInterval at totalETHBurn when missedIntervals+1 >= 10000/DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BURNING * INTERVALS_PER_DAY, causing amountAllocated for a single interval to equal the entire pool, making the 1.5% incentive apply to 100% of totalETHBurn instead of one interval's share
candidate.claimed_impact: Unprivileged caller extracts 1.5% of totalETHBurn (currently ~0.033 ETH, grows with distributeETHForBurning deposits) in a single call; remaining 98.5% is burned rather than retained, zeroing totalETHBurn

live_preconditions.name: timeElapsed since lastBurnedETHIntervalStartTimestamp >= 20 days (1728000 seconds)
live_preconditions.observed_value: NOT YET MET at capture time (~88109 seconds elapsed, ~24.5 hours). Attacker must wait ~19.5 additional days from block 25530134. Protocol has no liveness enforcement; precondition is achievable.

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Computes amountPerInterval = totalETHBurn * DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BURNING / BPS_DENOM / INTERVALS_PER_DAY, then _totalAmountForInterval = amountPerInterval * (missedIntervals + 1). Cap at line 633-635 clamps to totalETHBurn when missedIntervals+1 >= 2880 (20 days at 500 bps, 144 intervals/day). This is the root cause: the cap sets the single-interval allocation to the entire pool.
Calls _calculateIntervalsETHBurn and writes the capped _amountAllocated (= totalETHBurn) into ethIntervalsBurn[_lastInterval].amountAllocated before swapETHForTorusAndBurn reads it.

Reads currInterval.amountAllocated (= totalETHBurn after cap), computes incentive = amountAllocated * 150 / 10000, sends incentive ETH to msg.sender via .call{value: incentive}. The only replay guard (line 268) checks amountBurned != 0 for the current interval number, not the allocation size.
INCENTIVE_FEE=150, BPS_DENOM=10000, INTERVALS_PER_DAY=144, INTERVAL_TIME=600s. These are the fixed parameters used in the cap threshold and incentive calculation.

attacker_inputs: _deadline: any valid future uint32 timestamp (e.g. block.timestamp + 300)
call_sequence: 1. Wait until block.timestamp >= lastBurnedETHIntervalStartTimestamp + 1728000 (20 days = 2880 intervals * 600s)
state_before: ethIntervalsBurn[lastETHBurnIntervalNumber].amountBurned = 0 (guaranteed on first call to new interval)

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: Native ETH from contract balance (sourced from totalETHBurn accounting pool)
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: totalETHBurn * 150 / 10000 = 2218081612310240917 * 150 / 10000 = 33271224184653613 wei (~0.033 ETH at block 25530134 state; grows with future distributeETHForBurning calls)
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Protocol loses the entire totalETHBurn pool in one call: 1.5% to attacker, 98.5% burned via swap instead of being distributed across 2880 future intervals
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: Entitled incentive per legitimate call = amountPerInterval * 150 / 10000 = (totalETHBurn * 500 / 10000 / 144) * 150 / 10000 = totalETHBurn * 75000 / 144000000 = totalETHBurn / 1920 (~0.052%). Attacker receives totalETHBurn * 150 / 10000 = totalETHBurn / 66.67 (~1.5%), which is 1920/66.67 = ~28.8x the entitled amount per call. The cap converts a safety bound into an amplifier.

expected_assertions: assertEq(incentiveReceived, totalETHBurnBefore * 150 / 10000, 'incentive must equal 1.5% of full pool');
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If incentiveReceived < totalETHBurnBefore * 150 / 10000, the cap did not trigger; increase vm.warp offset beyond 20 days and retry. If still failing, verify DAILY_ALLOCATION_ETH_BURNING live value has not been changed by owner between block 25530134 and test execution.
